metadata.summary: A power of attorney (POA) ceases to be valid upon the death of the principal, leading to potential complications in managing assets. Understanding the limitations of POA is crucial for ensuring smooth transitions of wealth and avoiding probate. Beneficiary designations and payable on death accounts can provide alternatives to mitigate these issues.
metadata.why_it_matters: This matters for Kinage as it highlights the importance of proper estate planning for seniors to avoid complications after death. It also addresses the risk of fraud if POA is misused or misunderstood.
